heading: Destruction of veterans’ memorials
label: 1369 Destruction of veterans’ memorials
notes: Short Title of 2003 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–29, § 1117 Stat. 772May 29, 2003“This Act [enacting this section and provisions set out as a note under , Highways] may be cited as the ‘Veterans’ Memorial Preservation and Recognition Act of 2003’.”section 109 of Title 23
amendments: Short Title of 2003 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–29, § 1117 Stat. 772May 29, 2003“This Act [enacting this section and provisions set out as a note under , Highways] may be cited as the ‘Veterans’ Memorial Preservation and Recognition Act of 2003’.”section 109 of Title 23

children.label: a
children.text: Whoever, in a circumstance described in subsection (b), willfully injures or destroys, or attempts to injure or destroy, any structure, plaque, statue, or other monument on public property commemorating the service of any person or persons in the armed forces of the United States shall be fined under this title, imprisoned not more than 10 years, or both.

in committing the offense described in subsection (a), the defendant travels or causes another to travel in interstate or foreign commerce, or uses the mail or an instrumentality of interstate or foreign commerce; or
the structure, plaque, statue, or other monument described in subsection (a) is located on property owned by, or under the jurisdiction of, the Federal Government.
